description: Format the user guide draft at /home/ga/Documents/git_guide_draft.docx into a professional technical document by applying styles based on content markers. For paragraphs starting with [NOTE], [WARNING], or [TIP], remove the marker and apply a corresponding colored left border (>=1pt) and light background shading (blue, red/yellow, or green respectively). For paragraphs starting with [CODE], remove the marker and apply a monospace font with light gray background shading. Apply 'Heading 1' and 'Heading 2' styles to the document's sections and subsections. Save the final styled document to /home/ga/Documents/git_guide_styled.docx.

target_texts_note: Git stores data as a series of snapshots
The staging area is a file
Git does not explicitly track file movement
You can check the status of your files
target_texts_warning: If you lose your local .git directory
target_texts_tip: You can use git config --global alias
